description: Writes are not fsynced (or the directory is not synced after rename), so a crash loses or truncates the file.
body: Failure mode: code writes a file and returns, trusting that a successful write() means the data is on disk, or it renames a temp file without fsyncing the directory, so a crash or power loss before the page cache flushes leaves the file missing, truncated, or torn. Detection signals: data present before a crash is gone or partial after reboot; the bug only appears on hard power loss, not clean shutdown; no fsync in the write path. Do not equate a returned write with durability. Safer fallback: write to a temp file, fsync it, atomically rename over the target, fsync the containing directory, and verify with a crash-consistency test that injects a kill at each step.
